page: Microsoft Azure D12_v2 is a Dv2-family virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores running at 2.3 GHz with 1.0 thread per core. The memory configuration consists of 28.0 GB of RAM, providing 7.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 205 GB. The CPU architecture is x86_64, utilizing a 50 MB L3 cache and supporting AVX, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA instruction sets.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the D12_v2 exhibits modest processing capabilities. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng and Geekbench place the instance in the lower tier, with single-core metrics in the bottom 10% and multi-core metrics in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show weak read-only performance at 35.99 GB/sec for large blocks, though large block write bandwidth achieves an average rating at 29.34 GB/sec. Small-model LLM inference performs at an average level, delivering 399.48 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 87.48 tokens/sec for text generation.

Given its hardware profile, the D12_v2 offers a balanced resource density with a relatively high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7 GB per core, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads requiring more memory than raw compute power. However, the trade-off is its legacy CPU performance, which limits its suitability for compute-intensive tasks. This instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads, light database operations, basic web serving, and small-scale memory-bound applications that do not require high-frequency processing.
description: Microsoft Azure D12_v2 is a Dv2-family virtual machine configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. It features 28.0 GB of RAM and 205 GB of local storage, operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V. Benchmark data indicates modest performance, with single-core and multi-core CPU metrics falling into the lower percentiles. However, it maintains average performance in large-block memory write bandwidth and small-model LLM inference. Qualitatively, the D12_v2 provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a cost-efficient choice for memory-reliant workloads that do not demand high-performance compute. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, light database operations, and basic web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D12_v2 is a Dv2-family virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 28.0 GB of RAM, and 205 GB of local storage, designed for general-purpose and memory-balanced workloads.

tagline: A balanced virtual machine offering dedicated legacy Intel cores, generous memory allocation, and local storage for general-purpose workloads.
